description: 'nilua' is the hesitation or reserve that can arise under other people's attention, especially in unfamiliar company. A shy person may still want to join the conversation. 'luroi' (nervous) covers apprehension more broadly; 'nilua' keeps the social setting in view.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental nasal 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and move into high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', let air pass along its sides, then raise the back of the tongue for stressed rounded high-back 'u'. Final 'a' begins its own syllable after 'u'; unround the lips and open towards the central vowel.
usage_notes: The adjective can describe a person or their manner. A separate clause gives the person's next act; shyness itself stops at the hesitation.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Patient turn-taking gives a shy speaker room to enter when they are ready. A pause can then remain a pause instead of being guessed into agreement or refusal.

semantic_domains.community: reserve felt under other people's attention
